seo.title: IconBase - App Icon Generator for iOS, macOS, Favicons and PWA
seo.description: Generate Apple-style app icons online. Preview iOS, macOS, favicon, Windows and Android icon outputs, then export a complete icon package in one click.
seo.keywords: app icon generator, icon generator, ios app icon generator, favicon maker, mac icon creator, apple touch icon generator, pwa icon generator
seo.ogTitle: IconBase - Generate app icons for every platform
seo.ogDescription: Create Apple-style app icons, browser favicons, and export-ready files with live previews for iOS, macOS, Windows, Android, and browsers.
seo.twitterDescription: Turn a logo into polished app icons, favicons, Apple touch icons, and desktop icon assets in one browser-based tool.

hero.title: Create polished app icons for iOS, macOS, browsers, and PWAs
hero.intro: IconBase turns a single logo into Apple-style icon assets with live previews for browser tabs, mobile home screens, the macOS Dock, and desktop launch surfaces. No upload backend, no design software, and no manual packaging.

features.title: Real previews across key platforms
features.description: See how the same icon behaves as a favicon, Apple touch icon, Android home icon, macOS Dock icon, and Windows taskbar icon before you export.

features.title: Apple-style backgrounds and icon shaping
features.description: Combine Apple-inspired rounded rectangles with gradients, mesh backgrounds, glass effects, patterns, and customizable padding so the logo stays balanced at small sizes.

features.title: One-click export for production files
features.description: Generate PNG sizes, favicon.ico, Apple touch icon, Android Chrome assets, maskable icons, desktop PNGs, and a site.webmanifest in one package.

steps.title: Upload your logo
steps.description: Drop in a transparent PNG, SVG, JPG, or WEBP asset and keep everything inside the browser.

steps.title: Tune the visual treatment
steps.description: Pick a background preset, adjust padding, and set the corner radius until the icon reads clearly across the preview surfaces.

steps.title: Export the icon pack
steps.description: Download a ready-to-use zip that includes favicon, Apple, Android, desktop, and manifest files for immediate integration.

faq.question: Does IconBase upload my image to a server?
faq.answer: No. Icon rendering runs in the browser with Canvas, so your source image stays on your device.

faq.question: What files are included in the export package?
faq.answer: The zip includes favicon PNGs, favicon.ico, apple-touch-icon.png, Android Chrome PNGs, maskable icons, desktop PNGs, and a web manifest file.

faq.question: Can I use IconBase for web apps and native app mockups?
faq.answer: Yes. The generator is useful both for website favicon packs and for previewing how a logo could translate into iOS and macOS style app icons.

ios-app-icon-generator.generatorPreview: Watch the icon render into a realistic iPhone home screen alongside browser and desktop contexts for consistency checks.
ios-app-icon-generator.stepsTitle: How to make an iOS app icon

hero.eyebrow: iPhone and Apple touch icon workflow
hero.title: Generate iOS-style app icons with Apple-inspired rounding and live mobile previews
hero.intro: Use this page when your main goal is creating an iOS app icon, Apple touch icon, or polished iPhone home screen mockup. You can test padding, corner curvature, and visual weight before exporting a reusable icon pack.

features.title: Apple-style rounded icon treatment
features.description: Dial in the rounded square look that feels native on iPhone and iPad home screens without redrawing your original mark.

features.title: Live iPhone home screen preview
features.description: See your logo as an iOS-style app icon inside a realistic mobile layout so spacing and contrast issues are obvious early.

features.title: Export-ready Apple touch assets
features.description: The final zip includes apple-touch-icon.png and companion web icon files so the same design system can cover both web and app-facing use cases.

steps.title: Start from your logo
steps.description: Upload the brand mark or app glyph you want to test as an iOS icon.

steps.title: Balance shape and padding
steps.description: Adjust the padding and corner radius until the icon feels optically centered inside the rounded square.

steps.title: Export Apple-friendly outputs
steps.description: Download the packaged files and reuse the same icon system for web app installs, previews, and broader launch materials.

faq.question: Is this only for native iOS apps?
faq.answer: No. It is equally useful for Apple touch icons on websites, PWA install icons, and concept-stage app branding.

faq.question: Why is padding important for iOS icons?
faq.answer: A logo that fills too much of the rounded square often looks cramped on iPhone home screens, so padding helps preserve clarity and visual balance.

faq.question: Can I preview the icon before exporting?
faq.answer: Yes. The page renders your icon into mobile, browser, and desktop previews in real time before you generate the zip.

favicon-maker.cardDescription: Make favicon.ico, favicon PNGs, and browser-ready icon packs with instant tab preview.
favicon-maker.generatorIntro: Use this favicon-focused page when you care most about browser tab visibility, favicon.ico generation, and how your mark survives at 16x16 and 32x32 sizes.
favicon-maker.generatorControls: Test tiny-icon readability by switching backgrounds, tuning padding, and checking whether the mark still works at favicon scale.
favicon-maker.generatorPreview: The browser tab simulation helps you judge contrast and recognizability before you export favicon files.
favicon-maker.stepsTitle: How to make a favicon set
favicon-maker.relatedIntro: Need a more Apple-focused or desktop-focused version of the same workflow? These pages keep the generator but change the SEO and content angle.

seo.ogTitle: Favicon Maker - Generate favicon.ico and browser icons
seo.ogDescription: Create crisp favicon files with a live browser tab preview and export favicon.ico, PNG sizes, and related web app icons.
seo.twitterDescription: Generate browser favicon files, favicon.ico, and small-size icon assets with better preview control.

hero.title: Make favicon.ico and browser-ready favicon PNGs with live tab previews
hero.intro: This page is optimized for teams that need a clean favicon set for a website, SaaS app, or product launch page. Start from one logo, test readability at small sizes, and export the favicon package in one pass.

features.title: Preview browser tabs before export
features.description: Check whether the icon still feels recognizable at small favicon sizes in both light and dark tab environments.

features.title: Generate favicon.ico and PNG sizes
features.description: Export favicon.ico alongside standard 16x16 and 32x32 PNG favicon assets so modern and legacy browser scenarios are covered.

features.title: Keep website icons visually consistent
features.description: Use the same logo treatment across browser tabs, pinned shortcuts, Apple touch icons, and Android install icons instead of assembling files manually.

steps.title: Upload the website mark
steps.description: Drop in the symbol or compact logo version that should appear in browser tabs and bookmarks.

steps.title: Optimize for small sizes
steps.description: Adjust the icon padding and background so the favicon remains legible at 16x16 and 32x32.

steps.title: Download the favicon pack
steps.description: Export the zip and use the included favicon.ico, PNG, and manifest assets in your website head tags and app metadata.

faq.question: Does the export include favicon.ico?
faq.answer: Yes. The zip includes a generated favicon.ico file in addition to PNG favicon sizes.

faq.question: Why preview on a browser tab?
faq.answer: Favicons are usually viewed at very small sizes, so a browser tab preview shows whether the icon still reads clearly in real usage.

faq.question: Can I use the same export for a PWA?
faq.answer: Yes. The package also includes Apple touch, Android Chrome, and manifest files that help cover broader web app install scenarios.

hero.title: Create macOS-style app icons with live Dock previews and desktop-ready exports
hero.intro: Use this page when you want your logo to feel at home in the macOS Dock or desktop launch surfaces. You can shape the icon, tune contrast, and export larger desktop PNG assets from the same workspace.

features.title: Dock-focused desktop preview
features.description: Judge how the icon feels against a macOS-style Dock surface instead of guessing from a plain square canvas.

features.title: Apple-inspired desktop styling
features.description: Use rounded-square shapes, glass effects, gradients, and mesh treatments to give a simple logo more desktop polish.

features.title: Large export sizes for launch assets
features.description: The export bundle includes desktop PNG sizes such as 256x256 and 1024x1024 so you can reuse the same direction in presentations and product materials.

steps.title: Upload the app mark
steps.description: Start from the glyph, logo, or simplified brand mark that should become the desktop icon.

steps.title: Refine the desktop look
steps.description: Experiment with radius, glass, gradients, and spacing until the icon feels polished at Dock and desktop scales.

steps.title: Export the icon package
steps.description: Download the complete zip so the same Mac-friendly direction can also cover browser and mobile icon outputs.

faq.question: Can I use this for Mac app concept work?
faq.answer: Yes. The page is useful both for production-ready website icons and for exploring how a logo might translate into a Mac app icon concept.

faq.question: Does it export large desktop PNG files?
faq.answer: Yes. The package includes larger desktop-oriented PNG sizes that work well for presentations, previews, and launch assets.

faq.question: Will the same settings still work for web icons?
faq.answer: Yes. The shared generator exports browser, Apple, Android, and desktop files from the same icon state.
